subject: Project Management
title: When to Use Agile
description: A micro-lesson that makes When to Use Agile usable.

hook.text: Agile works best when requirements are unclear, technology is new, and the market is shifting.

buildup.text: Startups, new products, and innovation projects all face high uncertainty — exactly where agile excels.

discovery.text: Agile's short cycles and constant feedback help teams navigate uncertainty by learning as they build.

twist.text: Agile struggles in highly regulated environments where sequential documentation and traceability are mandatory.

climax.text: Agile is the right choice when the biggest risk is building the wrong thing — not building it wrong.

punchline.text: Don't know exactly what to build? Build a little and learn.

quiz.question: When is agile most effective?
options: When requirements are unclear and the market is changing
When there is no budget for testing
